name: Sant Hipòlit de Voltegrà and Sant Bartomeu del Grau – circular via the La Trona road
name_original: Sant Hipòlit de Voltegrà y Sant Bartomeu del Grau – circular por la carretera de La Trona
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Sant Hipòlit De Voltregà traverse a varied topography of gentle mountains, fertile valleys, and accessible paths. The region, situated in the Osona area of Catalonia, features diverse landscapes suitable for different cycling preferences. Cyclists encounter considerable slopes leading to viewpoints and cultural sites, alongside flatter routes along the Ter River. This blend of natural beauty and historical landmarks provides a dynamic environment for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Sant Hipòlit De Voltregà The most popular road cycling route is Sant Hipòlit de Voltegrà and Sant Bartomeu del Grau – circular via the La Trona road, a 38.3 miles (61.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 10 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a challenging circular ride through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Collsesplanes Pass – Revell Pass loop from Manlleu, a difficult 56.6 miles (91.0 km) path. This route features significant elevation gains as it loops through mountain passes from Manlleu. Local road cyclists also love the La Trona Road – La Trona Viewpoint loop from Manlleu, a 31.5 miles (50.6 km) trail leading through the La Trona Road and to the La Trona Viewpoint, often completed in about 2 hours 31 minutes. Road cycling around Sant Hipòlit De Voltregà is defined by gentle mountains, fertile valleys, and paths along the Ter River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging climbs to more relaxed rides. The routes in Sant Hipòlit De Voltregà are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 120 reviews. More than 13,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Sant Hipòlit De Voltregà's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Sant Hipòlit De Voltregà?
A: There are over 60 road cycling routes available around Sant Hipòlit De Voltregà, offering a diverse range of options for cyclists of all levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Sant Hipòlit De Voltregà?
A: The region offers a varied topography, from gentle mountains with lush vegetation to fertile valleys. You'll encounter considerable slopes leading to viewpoints and cultural sites, as well as flatter, smoother routes along the Ter River and its fluvial paths. This blend provides both challenging climbs and more relaxed rides.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Sant Hipòlit De Voltregà?
A: Sant Hipòlit De Voltregà is generally pleasant for cycling for much of the year. The varied topography means you can find suitable routes in different seasons, but spring and autumn typically offer the most comfortable temperatures and beautiful scenery.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Sant Hipòlit De Voltregà?
A: Yes, there are a few easier road cycling routes available. For instance, routes along the Ter River are known for being flatter and less strenuous, ideal for those seeking a more relaxed experience. Overall, 4 of the routes are rated as easy.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. Sant Hipòlit De Voltregà offers numerous challenging routes with significant elevation gains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26772256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collsesplanes Pass – Revell Pass loop from Manlleu&lt;/a&gt; features over 1,300 meters of elevation gain, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995726590/sant-hipolit-de-voltegra-and-sant-bartomeu-del-grau-circular-via-the-la-trona-road" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Hipòlit de Voltegrà and Sant Bartomeu del Grau – circular via the La Trona road&lt;/a&gt; includes over 1,000 meters of climbing. In total, 28 routes are rated as difficult.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Sant Hipòlit De Voltregà?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 120 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from challenging climbs to scenic river paths, and the blend of natural beauty with cultural landmarks.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Sant Hipòlit De Voltregà are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995726590/sant-hipolit-de-voltegra-and-sant-bartomeu-del-grau-circular-via-the-la-trona-road" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Hipòlit de Voltegrà and Sant Bartomeu del Grau – circular via the La Trona road&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a challenging loop through the local landscape.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: You can discover several natural features and viewpoints. The Creu de Morral is the highest point in the municipality, offering rewarding views. Other scenic spots include Font de la Sala and Sant Marc Viewpoint. You might also encounter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3054791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt d'en Nara Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3054793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Barra de Ferro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to visit while cycling?
A: Yes, the region is rich in cultural heritage. You can incorporate visits to the historic center of Sant Hipòlit, including the Sant Hipòlit church, or the nearby Sanctuary of La Gleva. Other notable sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5283613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Temple of Vic&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1837611" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Sant Pere de Casserres&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5412431" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vic Cathedral&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, several routes offer significant distances for long-distance cyclists. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/7792445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Bracons – Collada de Collfred loop from Manlleu&lt;/a&gt; covers over 94 kilometers, providing an extensive ride through the region's varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any specific routes that pass through mountain passes?
A: Yes, the region is known for its mountain passes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26772256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collsesplanes Pass – Revell Pass loop from Manlleu&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, taking you through significant mountain passes with rewarding climbs and descents.

Q: Can I find routes that connect to nearby towns?
A: The geographical location of Sant Hipòlit De Voltregà allows for easy connection to other nearby towns like Vic and Manlleu. Many routes start or pass through these towns, making it a strategic point for exploring the wider Osona region by bike.
tips: Small church with a beautiful garden, a good place to find shade and rest.

Town in the region of Osona. It is recommended to visit the Iglesia de San Baudilio (baroque) or the
Chapel of San Miguel de Vilaseca or Gallifa (Romanesque).

Soft climb with fantastic views. I prefer descenting it, as you really get to enjoy the landscape underneath you.

If you are finishing in Vic, then you can find a ton of small roads as alternatives to the main road. It makes for an easy roll down to Vic after a day on the bike.
